title: Ship narrative revision passes for arc consistency, promise/payoff, and voice drift
description:
## Problem
After first draft, writers struggle to run high-signal revision checks inside the app. Competitors help with drafting structure, but revision quality work (arc consistency, setup/payoff alignment, voice consistency) is still fragmented across external notes and manual checklists.

## Target user
Fiction writers entering revision phase who need guided, repeatable quality passes tied to manuscript structure.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase number of completed revision passes per active manuscript
- Reduce unresolved continuity/setup-payoff issues by final draft stage
- Increase manuscript completion rate after first-draft milestone

## Scope
- Add Revision Passes panel with three pass templates: Arc Consistency, Promise/Payoff, Voice Drift
- Each pass generates a checklist of scene-linked review items and statuses (open, accepted, dismissed)
- Allow writers to create custom notes per review item and mark final disposition
- Add pass summary view with unresolved items by chapter/scene
- Save revision history snapshots so writers can resume passes later

## Non-goals
- Auto-rewriting prose
- Grammar/spellcheck replacement
- General-purpose AI critique outside defined pass templates

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can start any pass type and receive scene-linked review items
- Writer can mark each item accepted/dismissed with required rationale note
- Summary view correctly counts unresolved items and deep-links to scene context
- Revision pass progress persists across reload and can be resumed later
- Writer can complete a pass and export a concise pass report

## Priority rationale
Market Research flagged revision intelligence as a differentiation gap. This feature moves beyond drafting parity and gives writers a practical quality workflow competitors under-serve.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

---

## Context from previous issue

[CEO]:
Assigned to Market Research.

[Market Research]:
Checkout release requested: 409

[Market Research]:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This pushes us past drafting parity into a revision system writers can actually execute: repeatable, scene-linked quality passes with explicit rationale and unresolved-item rollups. That is stronger than generic outlining/worldbuilding value because it operationalizes developmental editing inside the manuscript workflow.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter positions itself around "plotting, organization, smart story elements" and a smoother writing flow, which indicates strong drafting/structure emphasis rather than explicit guided revision passes (source: https://livingwriter.com/ ; also mirrored in page metadata on the public site).
- Campfire’s public positioning emphasizes "Write novels, worldbuild, and self-publish" plus modules such as word processor, worldbuilding tools, character creator, and story planner (source: https://www.campfirewriting.com/features/).
- Our own baseline in `docs/INSPIRATION.md` frames Living Writer around structure and Campfire around worldbuilding, with Inkitt focused on community feedback. This supports a differentiation move toward revision-intelligence workflows rather than only planning/community parity.

## Risks / trade-offs
- If pass templates are too rigid, advanced users may perceive them as checkbox busywork.
- Scene-linking and pass-history UX can add complexity unless design keeps the flow lightweight.
- Requires careful copy and defaults so users understand when to use Arc/Promise-Setup/Voice passes.

## Recommendation
Proceed, but require Design to keep pass start/resume flow to two clicks from manuscript context and make unresolved items visible without overwhelming the editor.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

[CEO]:
Board: please release checkout for this issue (assignee got 409). See docs/PAPERCLIP_SETUP.md § Release a stuck checkout.

[CEO]:
Assigned to Design.

[CEO]:
Board: please release checkout for this issue (assignee got 409). See docs/PAPERCLIP_SETUP.md § Release a stuck checkout.

[Design]:
## Design brief

## UX goal
Help writers run high-signal revision passes inside the editor without breaking drafting flow: start a pass in one click, process scene-linked items quickly, and always know what remains unresolved.

## Information hierarchy
1. Pass control layer: active pass type, progress, resume/start controls.
2. Current work queue: scene-linked review items with status + required rationale.
3. Scene context: quick jump into the related scene text.
4. Summary layer: unresolved counts by chapter/scene, export/report action, revision snapshot history.

## Layout and components
Desktop: keep existing split editor layout and repurpose right sidebar from generic Notes into a `Revision Passes` workspace with stacked sections:
- Pass selector row (Arc Consistency, Promise/Payoff, Voice Drift) as segmented controls.
- Active pass card (status chip, completion %, resume/start button).
- Review item list grouped by chapter with compact item rows (title, scene link, status select, rationale input trigger).
- Bottom summary module with unresolved totals and `View summary` / `Export pass report`.

Mobile/tablet: use a slide-over panel from right/bottom for Revision Passes so the editor remains primary; keep one-tap open/close from the editor header area.

## Interaction states
- Idle: no pass started; show short explanation + three pass cards with expected outcomes and `Start pass`.
- Loading: skeleton rows for pass summary and checklist items; keep scene editor interactive.
- Empty: pass exists but no flagged items yet; show confirmation copy and `Mark pass complete` affordance.
- Error: inline non-blocking alert in panel with retry action; preserve unsaved rationale text locally until retry succeeds.
- Success: lightweight confirmation bar after item disposition/save snapshot/export; auto-dismiss with persistent timestamp in history.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: full tab order across pass selector, item status controls, rationale inputs, and scene deep-links; Enter/Space parity for all custom controls.
- Focus: visible high-contrast focus outlines consistent with existing patterns; return focus to invoking control after closing mobile panel.
- Contrast: use token combinations at or above AA, especially muted text on `--color-polar-100`.
- Text clarity: replace jargon with writer language (e.g., “Unresolved setup/payoff items” instead of system wording).

## Visual style
Follow current grayscale system and editor restraint:
- Surfaces: `--color-white` primary, `--color-polar-100` secondary panel background.
- Borders/dividers: `--color-fashion-300`.
- Primary text: `--color-black-700`; secondary metadata: `--color-elephant-500` / `--color-cement-400`.
- Interactive emphasis: selected pass/state with `--color-fog-200` fill and `--color-carbon-600` focus/active accents.
No new accent color family needed for v1; differentiate states by iconography, label text, and density, not bright color noise.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop (>=1024): persistent right sidebar revision workspace beside editor.
- Tablet (768-1023): sidebar collapses to toggleable panel; preserve fast scene navigation and avoid horizontal compression of editor text column.
- Mobile (<768): full-height overlay panel for passes; one-thumb controls, sticky top actions (`Start/Resume`, `Summary`).

## Codebase alignment
Reviewed current structure and aligned proposal to existing primitives:
- Route/layout shell: `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`, `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`, `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx`.
- Shared structure: `src/components/Header/Header.tsx`, `src/components/ContentContainer/ContentContainer.tsx`.
- Existing control patterns: `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx`, scene list patterns from `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.tsx` and `src/components/SceneCard/SceneCard.tsx`.
- Token system: `src/styles/tokens.css`.

## Reuse vs new build
Reuse:
- Existing editor shell/grid and sidebar slot in story page.
- Existing segmented-control interaction style from ViewModeSwitch.
- Existing card/list affordance style language from SceneCard/Corkboard.

New build:
- `RevisionPassPanel` (sidebar/panel container).
- `RevisionPassSelector` (3-pass template selector).
- `RevisionChecklist` + `RevisionChecklistItem` (status + rationale workflow).
- `RevisionSummary` + `RevisionHistoryList` (unresolved rollup + snapshots/export entry).

## Feasibility notes
- Current scene model is minimal (`id/title/chapter/summary/status/order`), so pass items need a new data shape for disposition/rationale and scene linkage.
- Current story state is localStorage-backed; draft rationale text should be buffered to avoid data loss during transient failures.
- Required rationale on accepted/dismissed states should be enforced at component/form level before save action is enabled.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Keep initial integration inside `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` editor branch to avoid route sprawl in v1.
- Maintain current responsive behavior in `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`; add panel collapse behavior instead of replacing existing layout grid.
- Respect existing focus treatments and token usage from `src/components/Header/Header.module.css` and `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.module.css`.
- Preserve one-click mode switching (`Editor`/`Corkboard`) while adding revision controls; do not add multi-step mode gates.
- Quick-capture requirement: starting/resuming a pass should be <=2 clicks from active editor state.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

[CEO]:
Assigned to Founding Engineer.

<plan>

## Goal
Add a Revision Passes panel to the story editor sidebar with three guided pass templates (Arc Consistency, Promise/Payoff, Voice Drift), scene-linked checklist items with required rationale, pass summary with unresolved counts, and export of a pass report.

## Scope and files

**New files:**
- `src/types/revision.ts` — pass type, item, and pass data model
- `src/lib/revision.ts` — pass CRUD, item generation from scenes, localStorage persistence, export
- `src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionPassPanel.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionPassSelector.tsx`
- `src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionChecklist.tsx`
- `src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionChecklistItem.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionSummary.tsx`
- `src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionHistoryList.tsx`

**Modified files:**
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` — add revision pass state, sidebar tab integration
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` — extend sidebar styles for revision panel

## Step-by-step plan

1. **Define types** in `src/types/revision.ts`:
   ```ts
   export type PassType = 'arc-consistency' | 'promise-payoff' | 'voice-drift';
   export type ItemStatus = 'open' | 'accepted' | 'dismissed';
   export interface RevisionItem {
     id: string;
     passId: string;
     sceneId: string | null;
     chapter: string;
     sceneTitle: string;
     prompt: string;    // the review question for this item
     status: ItemStatus;
     rationale: string; // writer's note; required on accept/dismiss
   }
   export interface RevisionPass {
     id: string;
     storyId: string;
     type: PassType;
     startedAt: number;
     completedAt?: number;
     items: RevisionItem[];
   }
   ```

2. **Create `src/lib/revision.ts`**:
   - `REVISION_KEY = (storyId: string) => \`story-${storyId}-revision\``
   - `getPasses(storyId): RevisionPass[]` — load from localStorage, return `[]` if none
   - `getActivePass(storyId, type): RevisionPass | null` — most recent non-completed pass of given type
   - `createPass(storyId, type, scenes): RevisionPass`:
     - Generate items from scenes using pass-specific prompt templates (see below)
     - Add story-level items where scenes is empty
     - Save and return
   - `updateItem(storyId, passId, itemId, updates: Partial<RevisionItem>)` — patch item, persist
   - `completePass(storyId, passId)` — set `completedAt`, persist
   - `exportPassReport(pass: RevisionPass, storyTitle: string): string` — format as plain text:
     ```
     [Pass Type] Pass Report — [storyTitle]
     Started: [date]
     Completion: [%]
     
     === Chapter 1 ===
     [Scene Title] — [prompt]
     Status: Accepted | Rationale: [note]
     ...
     
     Unresolved: [count]
     ```
   - **Pass item generators** (pure functions, called by `createPass`):
     - `generateArcConsistencyItems(scenes)`: for each scene → prompt: `"Does '${scene.title}' directly serve the story's central arc? Does the scene outcome move the protagonist forward or backward?"`. Story-level item: `"Does the overall story arc have a clear escalation from Act 1 to Act 3?"`
     - `generatePromisePayoffItems(scenes)`: for each scene → prompt: `"Does '${scene.title}' plant a setup that pays off later, or deliver a payoff for an earlier promise?"`. Chapter-level item per unique chapter: `"Are all setups in ${chapter} paid off by the final chapter?"`
     - `generateVoiceDriftItems(scenes)`: for scenes with `pov` set → prompt: `"Does '${scene.title}' maintain consistent voice and POV for ${scene.pov}?"`. For scenes without POV: `"Who is the POV character in '${scene.title}'? Is the voice consistent with surrounding scenes?"`

3. **Create `RevisionPassSelector`** (`src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionPassSelector.tsx`):
   - Renders 3 pass cards when no pass is active for a type, or an active-pass row when one exists
   - Each card: pass name, one-line description of what it checks, `Start pass` button
   - If an in-progress pass exists: show status chip (e.g., "In progress · 3/12 items resolved") and `Resume` button
   - If a completed pass exists: show `Completed` chip with date and `New pass` button
   - Segmented control style matching `ViewModeSwitch` visual language

4. **Create `RevisionChecklist`** and `RevisionChecklistItem`:
   - `RevisionChecklist`: groups items by chapter; renders chapter heading + list of `RevisionChecklistItem`
   - `RevisionChecklistItem`: 
     - Scene link button (label: scene title, chapter) — calls `onSceneJump(sceneId)` which switches to corkboard view
     - Status select: `<select>` or custom button group with values `open | accepted | dismissed` (with `aria-label`)
     - Rationale `<textarea>`: required before `accepted` or `dismissed` can be saved (show inline "Note required" error if empty); auto-expand to content
     - Save button per item (explicit save, not auto-save, to match "required rationale" contract)
     - Saved rationale shown as read-only text with "Edit" affordance
     - Keyboard: full tab order through status control → rationale → save

5. **Create `RevisionSummary`**:
   - Props: `pass`, `onComplete`, `onExport`
   - Shows: unresolved count by chapter (chapter name + count), total accepted/dismissed/open
   - "Mark pass complete" button (disabled until all items resolved or explicitly overridden)
   - "Export pass report" button → calls `exportPassReport` → creates a `Blob` with `text/plain` MIME type → `URL.createObjectURL` → triggers `<a download="revision-pass.txt">` click → revokes URL
   - Compact card, stays at bottom of sidebar

6. **Create `RevisionHistoryList`**:
   - Collapsible section "Previous passes" listing completed passes (type, date, completion %)
   - Clicking a completed pass shows read-only view of its items and rationale

7. **Create `RevisionPassPanel` container** (`RevisionPassPanel.tsx`):
   - State: `activePassType: PassType | null` (which pass type is selected/active)
   - Renders: pass selector row → active pass checklist → summary module
   - Loading skeleton: 3 skeleton rows while pass items are loading from localStorage
   - Error state: inline alert in panel with retry
   - Mobile: panel becomes a slide-over via `position: fixed` when sidebar is hidden; trigger button in `.editorHeader`

8. **Update `page.tsx`**:
   - Import `RevisionPassPanel`, add to sidebar mode state: `'notes' | 'history' | 'readAloud' | 'revision'`
   - Add sidebar tab bar with tabs (check if prior BIN-93/BIN-95 already added this; if so, extend; if not, add now)
   - Add "Revision" tab to sidebar tab bar
   - Pass `scenes` (from existing `scenes` state), `storyId`, and `onSceneJump` callback to `RevisionPassPanel`
   - `onSceneJump(sceneId)`: switch `viewMode` to `'corkboard'` (scene context jump); Note to Code Monkey: corkboard scene scroll/highlight is a future enhancement; for v1, just switching to corkboard is sufficient
   - Mobile: add "Revision" icon button to `.editorHeader` that opens the panel as a slide-over

9. **Update `page.module.css`**:
   - Sidebar tab bar (`.sidebarTabBar`) — if not yet added by prior features, add now: horizontal flex, tab buttons with active underline style
   - `.revisionPanel` — flex column, full sidebar height, overflow-y auto
   - `.passCard` — card with border `--color-fashion-300`, bg `--color-white`, padding `--spacing-md`
   - `.passStatusChip` — small inline label; `border: 1px solid var(--color-fashion-300)`; no fill color
   - `.checklistItem` — flex layout: scene link left, status/rationale right; border-bottom `--color-fashion-300`
   - `.rationaleField` — full-width textarea, border `--color-fashion-300`, focus outline `--color-carbon-600`
   - `.summaryModule` — sticky bottom in panel, `--color-polar-100` background, border-top
   - All tokens only from `src/styles/tokens.css`

## Test plan

- **Start pass**: click "Start pass" (Arc Consistency) → items generated from scenes (one per scene + story-level items); checklist renders grouped by chapter
- **Item acceptance**: select "Accepted" status → "Note required" error if rationale empty → add rationale → Save → item shows accepted state
- **Dismissal**: dismiss an item → rationale required → saved; item marked as dismissed
- **Unresolved counts**: summary module shows correct counts per chapter; updates after each item save
- **Complete pass**: all items resolved → "Mark pass complete" enables → click → pass marked complete, timestamp set
- **Export**: click "Export pass report" → text file downloads with correct content
- **Resume pass**: navigate away and back → existing in-progress pass loads; previous rationale intact
- **Multiple pass types**: start Promise/Payoff while Arc Consistency is in progress → separate passes maintained
- **Scene jump**: click scene link on item → view switches to corkboard
- **Keyboard**: Tab order through all interactive elements; status select and rationale accessible
- **Mobile**: revision panel accessible via slide-over; one-tap open/close from header

## Risks and mitigations

- **Rationale enforcement**: status save is blocked without rationale — validate at save time, not on blur, to avoid premature error messages
- **localStorage size**: passes store full item text; with many scenes and multiple passes, size could grow. v1 limit: 10 passes per story (oldest trimmed on `createPass`)
- **Scene data dependency**: if no scenes exist (fresh story), items will be story-level-only. Handle gracefully: show "Add scenes in the Corkboard to get scene-linked items"
- **Sidebar tab conflicts**: if BIN-93 or BIN-95 already added a sidebar tab bar, extend that exact component rather than creating a new one to avoid duplicate tab bars

## Rollback strategy
All code is additive. Remove the "Revision" sidebar tab from `page.tsx` to fully disable access. Existing story data is not affected.

## Observability
- `console.log('RevisionPass: created', { passId, type, itemCount, storyId })`
- `console.log('RevisionPass: item updated', { passId, itemId, status })`
- `console.log('RevisionPass: completed', { passId, type })`
- `console.log('RevisionPass: exported report')`

## Acceptance mapping
- "Writer can start any pass type and receive scene-linked review items" → `createPass` generates items from scenes; `RevisionChecklist` shows them
- "Writer can mark each item accepted/dismissed with required rationale note" → `RevisionChecklistItem` enforces rationale before save
- "Summary view correctly counts unresolved items and deep-links to scene context" → `RevisionSummary` + `onSceneJump`
- "Revision pass progress persists across reload and can be resumed later" → localStorage-backed `getActivePass`
- "Writer can complete a pass and export a concise pass report" → `completePass` + `exportPassReport` text download

## Handoff

Assign to: Code Monkey


</plan>

title: Add scene-level collaboration inbox with decision tracking for co-authors and editors
description:
## Problem
The app currently supports solo drafting but lacks a structured way to collaborate with a co-author or developmental editor. Writers fall back to email/docs comments, lose decisions, and break manuscript flow.

## Target user
Solo writers working with one trusted collaborator (co-author, editor, or critique partner) who need feedback and approvals tied to specific scenes.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase percentage of active manuscripts with at least one collaborator interaction
- Reduce median time from feedback request to accepted scene decision
- Improve retention in revision-stage users

## Scope
- Add per-scene collaboration thread with comment types: question, suggestion, decision
- Add assignee and status for each thread (open, needs-author, resolved)
- Add manuscript-level Collaboration Inbox that aggregates unresolved scene threads
- Add decision log entry when author marks a suggestion accepted/rejected, linked to scene and timestamp
- Add notify badge for unresolved collaborator items in writer workspace

## Non-goals
- Real-time multi-cursor editing
- Public/social community feed
- Billing, team-seat management, or organization admin controls

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can create and resolve scene-linked collaboration threads
- Each thread supports assignment and status transitions without losing history
- Collaboration Inbox lists all unresolved threads and deep-links to exact scene context
- Accept/reject actions create immutable decision log records with actor and timestamp
- Writer can filter inbox by assignee and status

## Priority rationale
Market Research highlighted collaboration as a major competitor gap. Shipping a manuscript-specific collaboration inbox creates clear differentiation from solo-first tools while supporting real revision workflows.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

---

## Context from previous issue

[CEO]:
Assigned to Market Research.

[Market Research]:
Checkout release requested: 409

[Market Research]:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This stands out by focusing collaboration on scene-level decisions with explicit ownership and an immutable decision trail. Competitors emphasize sharing/collab access, but this ticket targets the revision bottleneck: turning feedback into trackable decisions without losing manuscript context.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter markets sharing/co-authoring at manuscript/chapter level ("effortless sharing and co-authoring"), but does not clearly position a scene-scoped decision inbox workflow as a core UX: https://livingwriter.com/en
- LivingWriter’s June 17, 2025 changelog says expanded collaboration features (tracked changes, live cursors, comments, in-document chat) were upcoming, indicating movement toward broad collaboration capabilities: https://guides.livingwriter.com/whats-new/changelog/june-17-2025
- Campfire highlights inviting collaborators to view/comment/edit drafts, with no explicit manuscript decision-tracking inbox concept in core positioning: https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- Inkitt positions collaboration primarily as reader/community feedback loops (wall posts, comments, reviews) rather than private editor/co-author decision workflows: https://www.inkitt.com/writers

## Risks / trade-offs
- Could drift into a generic comments feature unless the decision log and inbox triage remain first-class.
- Added workflow states may increase cognitive load for solo writers unless defaults are lightweight.
- Competitors may close the gap on collaboration quickly; differentiation depends on execution quality and clarity.

## Recommendation
Approve with a design requirement: make the Collaboration Inbox feel like a "revision command center" (fast triage, clear ownership, and one-click jump to scene context), and make accepted/rejected decisions visible as narrative memory, not just comment history.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

[CEO]:
Assigned to Design.

[CEO]:
Board: please release checkout for this issue (assignee got 409). See docs/PAPERCLIP_SETUP.md § Release a stuck checkout.

[Design]:
## UX goal
Create a writer-first "revision command center" that keeps feedback tied to scene context, makes ownership/status obvious at a glance, and turns collaborator suggestions into durable decisions without breaking drafting flow.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: unresolved collaboration work (what needs attention now).
2. Secondary: scene context and thread details (who said what, where, and why).
3. Tertiary: immutable decision history (accepted/rejected with actor and timestamp).
4. Utility: filters (assignee/status/type) and quick jump back to writing surface.

## Layout and components
- Keep collaboration inside the existing story workspace (`/story/[id]`) so context is never lost.
- In editor mode, repurpose the current right sidebar from "Notes" to a tabbed utility panel: `Collaboration` (default when unresolved items exist) and `Notes`.
- `Collaboration` tab structure:
- Top: compact inbox toolbar (unresolved count, status filter, assignee filter, scene filter).
- Middle: thread list grouped by status (`Open`, `Needs author`, `Resolved`) with per-row chips for type (`Question`, `Suggestion`, `Decision`) and assignee.
- Bottom/detail pane or expandable row: full thread timeline + actions (`Assign`, `Set status`, `Accept`, `Reject`, `Mark resolved`).
- Add a sticky "Jump to scene" action on each thread row to keep scene-linked deep navigation one-click.
- Add a manuscript-level decision log view as a secondary panel state in the same sidebar (toggle: `Inbox | Decision log`) rather than a new top-level route in v1.
- Show unresolved badge in writer workspace chrome: in `Header` actions area and as a small indicator on the `Collaboration` tab label when inside a story.

## Interaction states
- Idle: sorted unresolved list (priority: `Needs author` first, then `Open`, newest activity first).
- Loading: skeleton rows matching thread card structure; keep filters visible but disabled.
- Empty: two variants. `No unresolved threads` (encourage return to drafting) and `No results for current filters` (clear-filter CTA).
- Error: inline non-blocking banner in panel with retry; preserve current filter selections.
- Success: lightweight inline confirmation on decision actions (`Accepted`/`Rejected` logged) plus immediate list/count update.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: full tab order for filters, rows, action menu, and "Jump to scene"; Enter/Space opens thread details.
- Focus: use visible focus rings consistent with existing components; maintain focus after state-changing actions.
- Contrast: use approved grayscale tokens only; badges/chips must maintain readable contrast against `--color-white` and `--color-polar-100`.
- Text clarity: plain-language labels (`Needs author`, `Assigned to you`, `Accepted by you 2h ago`) over jargon.

## Visual style
- Stay within existing grayscale system (`src/styles/tokens.css`): panel backgrounds on `--color-polar-100`, cards on `--color-white`, borders on `--color-fashion-300`, primary text on `--color-black-700`, secondary text on `--color-elephant-500`/`--color-cement-400`.
- Keep visual density moderate: card-based rows with clear spacing (`--spacing-sm` to `--spacing-md`) and quiet hierarchy.
- Use shape/weight/placement (not bright color) to distinguish thread type and urgency.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop/tablet: collaboration sidebar visible in editor layout (existing 2-column structure).
- Mobile (<768px): sidebar becomes a slide-over panel launched from header action; preserve one-tap return to editor.
- Mobile thread rows collapse metadata to two lines max with overflow-safe truncation; actions move into a single "More" menu.

## Codebase alignment
Reviewed and aligned with current architecture:
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` and `page.module.css` (existing editor + right sidebar shell).
- `src/components/AppShell/*`, `Header/*`, `Navigation/*`, `ContentContainer/*` (global chrome + action/badge anchor points).
- `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/*`, `Corkboard/*`, `SceneCard/*` (current interaction and card/list conventions).
- `src/styles/tokens.css` (approved grayscale and spacing/typography primitives).

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse: existing story editor layout shell, sidebar container area, header action slot, card and toolbar visual language, focus-outline patterns, token palette.
- New build: collaboration-specific thread list item, filter row, decision action strip, and decision-log list inside story sidebar context.

## Feasibility notes
- Decision records must be immutable in UI presentation once created; edits should appear as new timeline events, not overwritten rows.
- Thread status and assignee changes need optimistic UI with rollback path for failed saves.
- "Jump to scene" requires stable scene/thread linkage; if target scene is missing, show clear fallback state in thread detail.
- Notify badge count should reflect unresolved items only and refresh after every thread mutation.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Anchor collaboration panel work in `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` sidebar area where `Notes` currently renders.
- Use existing sticky/header behavior patterns from `src/components/Header/Header.tsx` and `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.module.css` toolbar style.
- Keep styles token-driven in local CSS modules; do not introduce non-token colors.
- For v1, avoid new global navigation routes; keep manuscript-level inbox scoped to current story workspace to reduce structural churn.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

<plan>

## Goal
Add a scene-level Collaboration panel to the story editor sidebar: create and manage typed comment threads per scene, aggregate unresolved threads in a manuscript-level inbox, record accept/reject decisions as an immutable log, and surface an unresolved badge in the workspace.

## Scope and files

**New files:**
- `src/types/collaboration.ts` — thread, comment, and decision type definitions
- `src/lib/collaboration.ts` — thread CRUD, comment/decision operations, localStorage persistence
- `src/components/CollabPanel/CollabPanel.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/CollabPanel/CollabInboxToolbar.tsx`
- `src/components/CollabPanel/CollabThreadList.tsx`
- `src/components/CollabPanel/CollabThreadRow.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/CollabPanel/CollabThreadDetail.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/CollabPanel/AddThreadModal.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/CollabPanel/CollabBadge.tsx`

**Modified files:**
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` — add collaboration state, sidebar tab integration, badge in header
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` — extend sidebar styles for collaboration panel

## Step-by-step plan

1. **Define types** in `src/types/collaboration.ts`:
   ```ts
   export type ThreadType = 'question' | 'suggestion' | 'decision';
   export type ThreadStatus = 'open' | 'needs-author' | 'resolved';
   export interface CollabComment {
     id: string;
     author: string; // free-text name
     body: string;
     createdAt: number;
   }
   export interface DecisionRecord {
     action: 'accepted' | 'rejected';
     actor: string; // name
     note: string;
     timestamp: number;
   }
   export interface CollabThread {
     id: string;
     storyId: string;
     sceneId: string | null; // null = story-level
     sceneTitle: string;     // "Story-level" when sceneId is null
     type: ThreadType;
     title: string;
     status: ThreadStatus;
     assignee: string;       // name (free-text for v1)
     comments: CollabComment[];
     decision?: DecisionRecord;
     createdAt: number;
     updatedAt: number;
   }
   ```

2. **Create `src/lib/collaboration.ts`**:
   - `COLLAB_KEY = (storyId: string) => \`story-${storyId}-collab\``
   - `getThreads(storyId): CollabThread[]` — load from localStorage, sorted by status priority (`needs-author` first, then `open`, then `resolved`), then by `updatedAt` desc
   - `createThread(storyId, fields: Omit<CollabThread, 'id' | 'comments' | 'createdAt' | 'updatedAt'>): CollabThread`
   - `updateThread(storyId, threadId, updates: Partial<Pick<CollabThread, 'status' | 'assignee' | 'title'>>)`: patch + refresh `updatedAt`
   - `addComment(storyId, threadId, comment: Omit<CollabComment, 'id' | 'createdAt'>): CollabComment`
   - `recordDecision(storyId, threadId, decision: Omit<DecisionRecord, 'timestamp'>)`: sets `thread.decision`, sets `thread.status = 'resolved'`, refreshes `updatedAt`
   - `getUnresolvedCount(storyId): number` — count threads where `status !== 'resolved'`
   - Helper: `filterThreads(threads, filters: { assignee?: string, status?: ThreadStatus[], sceneId?: string | null }): CollabThread[]`

3. **Create `CollabInboxToolbar`**:
   - Props: `unresolvedCount`, `statusFilter`, `assigneeFilter`, `sceneFilter`, `onFilterChange`
   - Shows: "X unresolved" count chip, status filter buttons (All | Open | Needs Author | Resolved), assignee filter (free-text input or dropdown of known assignees), scene filter (dropdown of scene titles from `scenes` prop)
   - Compact horizontal layout with spacing tokens

4. **Create `CollabThreadRow`**:
   - Shows: type chip (`Question` | `Suggestion` | `Decision`), thread title (truncated), status chip, assignee name, scene name, relative timestamp
   - Actions row: "Jump to scene" (calls `onSceneJump(sceneId)`), status quick-change buttons (`Set → Open | Needs author | Resolved`)
   - Click anywhere on row body → expand to `CollabThreadDetail`
   - Keyboard: `role="button"`, Enter/Space to expand; action buttons have separate tab stops

5. **Create `CollabThreadDetail`**:
   - Full thread view: thread title, type, scene link, assignee
   - Comment timeline: each `CollabComment` with author, body (markdown-safe, rendered as text), relative timestamp
   - "Add comment" form: author name input + body textarea + Submit button
   - Decision section (only for `suggestion` threads):
     - "Accept" / "Reject" buttons (only shown when status is `open` or `needs-author`)
     - On click: shows inline note input → confirm → calls `recordDecision`
     - After decision: immutable decision block shows action, actor, note, timestamp (cannot be edited/deleted)
   - Status change: compact select for `open | needs-author | resolved` at top of detail
   - Assign: editable assignee name field
   - Decision log display: if `thread.decision` exists, show it prominently

6. **Create `AddThreadModal`**:
   - Fields: Thread type (Question | Suggestion | Decision — button group), Title, Assignee (free-text), Scene (dropdown from scenes list, with "Story-level" option), optional first comment body
   - Validation: title required, type required
   - Submit → calls `createThread`, closes modal, refreshes list
   - Keyboard: focus trap, Escape closes without save

7. **Create `CollabBadge`**:
   - Small round badge showing unresolved count
   - Props: `count: number`; renders nothing if `count === 0`
   - Used in sidebar tab label and optionally in story editor header

8. **Create `CollabPanel` container** (`CollabPanel.tsx`):
   - State: `activeThreadId: string | null` (which thread is expanded), `showAddModal`, filters
   - On mount: load threads from `getThreads(storyId)`; refresh after any mutation
   - Renders: `CollabInboxToolbar` + `CollabThreadList` (filtered list of `CollabThreadRow`)
   - When `activeThreadId` set: renders `CollabThreadDetail` below list (or replaces list on mobile)
   - Toggle: "Inbox | Decision Log" — decision log view shows only resolved threads with `decision` records, read-only
   - "Add thread" CTA button → opens `AddThreadModal`
   - Loading skeleton: 3 skeleton thread rows
   - Empty states: "No unresolved threads — back to drafting" and "No results for current filters" with clear-filter CTA

9. **Update `page.tsx`**:
   - Import `CollabPanel`, `CollabBadge`
   - Load `unresolvedCount = getUnresolvedCount(storyId)` (refresh when panel state changes)
   - Add `'collab'` to sidebar mode state
   - Add "Collab" tab with `CollabBadge` badge to sidebar tab bar
   - Pass `scenes` (for scene picker) and `onSceneJump` to `CollabPanel`
   - Optionally show `CollabBadge` in `.editorHeader` next to existing controls (subtle badge on unresolved > 0)
   - Mobile: "Collab" opens as slide-over panel from header

10. **Update `page.module.css`**:
    - Extend sidebar tab bar (add Collab tab)
    - `.collabPanel` — flex column, full sidebar height
    - `.threadRow` — card-style row with border-bottom, hover state `--color-fog-200`
    - `.typeChip` / `.statusChip` — inline text badges with border, no color fill
    - `.threadDetail` — slightly inset card in sidebar
    - `.commentItem` — compact comment block with author (bold), body, timestamp
    - `.decisionBlock` — `--color-polar-100` background with border, immutable appearance
    - `.addThreadModal` — centered dialog overlay (`--z-modal`), focus trap, `--color-white` card
    - `.collabBadge` — small round badge, border + count text, `--color-carbon-600` text
    - All tokens from `src/styles/tokens.css` only

## Test plan

- **Create thread**: click "Add thread" → fill Question type, title "Is the opening scene too slow?", assignee "Editor", scene "Opening Scene" → Submit → thread appears in inbox
- **Thread types**: create Question, Suggestion, Decision threads → correct type chips shown
- **Add comment**: expand thread → add comment with author + body → comment appears in timeline with timestamp
- **Status change**: set thread to "Needs author" → moves to top of priority sort; set to "Resolved" → moves to bottom
- **Accept/Reject decision**: expand suggestion thread → click "Accept" → add note → confirm → decision block appears (immutable); status becomes Resolved
- **Decision log**: switch to Decision Log view → only resolved threads with decisions shown, read-only
- **Inbox filters**: filter by status → list updates; filter by assignee → shows matching threads; clear filter → all back
- **Scene jump**: click "Jump to scene" on a thread → corkboard view opens
- **Badge**: unresolved threads → badge shows count in sidebar tab and header; resolve all → badge disappears
- **Persistence**: refresh page → all threads, comments, and decisions preserved from localStorage
- **Keyboard**: Tab through toolbar → thread rows → action buttons; Enter opens thread; AddThreadModal focus trap; Escape closes modal
- **Mobile**: verify panel accessible as slide-over; thread rows truncate to 2 lines

## Risks and mitigations

- **No real-time sync**: v1 is single-user localhost. Decision log is append-only immutable in localStorage. Note clearly in code.
- **Assignee as free text**: no validation of assignee names in v1. Consistent naming is writer's responsibility.
- **localStorage size with many comments**: limit comment body to 1000 chars via `maxLength` attribute on textarea; soft warning if thread count exceeds 100
- **Sidebar tab crowding**: if BIN-83/BIN-93/BIN-95 added tabs too, the tab bar may get crowded. Use short tab labels ("Collab", "History", "Revision", "Read") with full labels in `aria-label`

## Rollback strategy
Remove "Collab" tab from sidebar tab bar in `page.tsx` to disable access. `getThreads` returns `[]` if key missing. Existing story data unaffected.

## Observability
- `console.log('Collab: thread created', { threadId, type, storyId })`
- `console.log('Collab: decision recorded', { threadId, action, storyId })`

## Acceptance mapping
- "Writer can create and resolve scene-linked collaboration threads" → `createThread` + `updateThread` status
- "Each thread supports assignment and status transitions without losing history" → `CollabThreadDetail` with comment timeline + immutable decision record
- "Collaboration Inbox lists all unresolved threads and deep-links to exact scene context" → `CollabPanel` + `onSceneJump`
- "Accept/reject actions create immutable decision log records with actor and timestamp" → `recordDecision` in `collaboration.ts`; `DecisionRecord` is never mutated after creation
- "Writer can filter inbox by assignee and status" → `CollabInboxToolbar` with filters

## Handoff

Assign to: Code Monkey


</plan>

title: Add scene-level collaboration inbox with decision tracking for co-authors and editors
description:
## Problem
The app currently supports solo drafting but lacks a structured way to collaborate with a co-author or developmental editor. Writers fall back to email/docs comments, lose decisions, and break manuscript flow.

## Target user
Solo writers working with one trusted collaborator (co-author, editor, or critique partner) who need feedback and approvals tied to specific scenes.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase percentage of active manuscripts with at least one collaborator interaction
- Reduce median time from feedback request to accepted scene decision
- Improve retention in revision-stage users

## Scope
- Add per-scene collaboration thread with comment types: question, suggestion, decision
- Add assignee and status for each thread (open, needs-author, resolved)
- Add manuscript-level Collaboration Inbox that aggregates unresolved scene threads
- Add decision log entry when author marks a suggestion accepted/rejected, linked to scene and timestamp
- Add notify badge for unresolved collaborator items in writer workspace

## Non-goals
- Real-time multi-cursor editing
- Public/social community feed
- Billing, team-seat management, or organization admin controls

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can create and resolve scene-linked collaboration threads
- Each thread supports assignment and status transitions without losing history
- Collaboration Inbox lists all unresolved threads and deep-links to exact scene context
- Accept/reject actions create immutable decision log records with actor and timestamp
- Writer can filter inbox by assignee and status

## Priority rationale
Market Research highlighted collaboration as a major competitor gap. Shipping a manuscript-specific collaboration inbox creates clear differentiation from solo-first tools while supporting real revision workflows.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

---

## Context from previous issue

[CEO]:
Assigned to Market Research.

[Market Research]:
Checkout release requested: 409

[Market Research]:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This stands out by focusing collaboration on scene-level decisions with explicit ownership and an immutable decision trail. Competitors emphasize sharing/collab access, but this ticket targets the revision bottleneck: turning feedback into trackable decisions without losing manuscript context.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter markets sharing/co-authoring at manuscript/chapter level ("effortless sharing and co-authoring"), but does not clearly position a scene-scoped decision inbox workflow as a core UX: https://livingwriter.com/en
- LivingWriter’s June 17, 2025 changelog says expanded collaboration features (tracked changes, live cursors, comments, in-document chat) were upcoming, indicating movement toward broad collaboration capabilities: https://guides.livingwriter.com/whats-new/changelog/june-17-2025
- Campfire highlights inviting collaborators to view/comment/edit drafts, with no explicit manuscript decision-tracking inbox concept in core positioning: https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- Inkitt positions collaboration primarily as reader/community feedback loops (wall posts, comments, reviews) rather than private editor/co-author decision workflows: https://www.inkitt.com/writers

## Risks / trade-offs
- Could drift into a generic comments feature unless the decision log and inbox triage remain first-class.
- Added workflow states may increase cognitive load for solo writers unless defaults are lightweight.
- Competitors may close the gap on collaboration quickly; differentiation depends on execution quality and clarity.

## Recommendation
Approve with a design requirement: make the Collaboration Inbox feel like a "revision command center" (fast triage, clear ownership, and one-click jump to scene context), and make accepted/rejected decisions visible as narrative memory, not just comment history.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

[CEO]:
Assigned to Design.

[CEO]:
Board: please release checkout for this issue (assignee got 409). See docs/PAPERCLIP_SETUP.md § Release a stuck checkout.

[Design]:
## UX goal
Create a writer-first "revision command center" that keeps feedback tied to scene context, makes ownership/status obvious at a glance, and turns collaborator suggestions into durable decisions without breaking drafting flow.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: unresolved collaboration work (what needs attention now).
2. Secondary: scene context and thread details (who said what, where, and why).
3. Tertiary: immutable decision history (accepted/rejected with actor and timestamp).
4. Utility: filters (assignee/status/type) and quick jump back to writing surface.

## Layout and components
- Keep collaboration inside the existing story workspace (`/story/[id]`) so context is never lost.
- In editor mode, repurpose the current right sidebar from "Notes" to a tabbed utility panel: `Collaboration` (default when unresolved items exist) and `Notes`.
- `Collaboration` tab structure:
- Top: compact inbox toolbar (unresolved count, status filter, assignee filter, scene filter).
- Middle: thread list grouped by status (`Open`, `Needs author`, `Resolved`) with per-row chips for type (`Question`, `Suggestion`, `Decision`) and assignee.
- Bottom/detail pane or expandable row: full thread timeline + actions (`Assign`, `Set status`, `Accept`, `Reject`, `Mark resolved`).
- Add a sticky "Jump to scene" action on each thread row to keep scene-linked deep navigation one-click.
- Add a manuscript-level decision log view as a secondary panel state in the same sidebar (toggle: `Inbox | Decision log`) rather than a new top-level route in v1.
- Show unresolved badge in writer workspace chrome: in `Header` actions area and as a small indicator on the `Collaboration` tab label when inside a story.

## Interaction states
- Idle: sorted unresolved list (priority: `Needs author` first, then `Open`, newest activity first).
- Loading: skeleton rows matching thread card structure; keep filters visible but disabled.
- Empty: two variants. `No unresolved threads` (encourage return to drafting) and `No results for current filters` (clear-filter CTA).
- Error: inline non-blocking banner in panel with retry; preserve current filter selections.
- Success: lightweight inline confirmation on decision actions (`Accepted`/`Rejected` logged) plus immediate list/count update.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: full tab order for filters, rows, action menu, and "Jump to scene"; Enter/Space opens thread details.
- Focus: use visible focus rings consistent with existing components; maintain focus after state-changing actions.
- Contrast: use approved grayscale tokens only; badges/chips must maintain readable contrast against `--color-white` and `--color-polar-100`.
- Text clarity: plain-language labels (`Needs author`, `Assigned to you`, `Accepted by you 2h ago`) over jargon.

## Visual style
- Stay within existing grayscale system (`src/styles/tokens.css`): panel backgrounds on `--color-polar-100`, cards on `--color-white`, borders on `--color-fashion-300`, primary text on `--color-black-700`, secondary text on `--color-elephant-500`/`--color-cement-400`.
- Keep visual density moderate: card-based rows with clear spacing (`--spacing-sm` to `--spacing-md`) and quiet hierarchy.
- Use shape/weight/placement (not bright color) to distinguish thread type and urgency.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop/tablet: collaboration sidebar visible in editor layout (existing 2-column structure).
- Mobile (<768px): sidebar becomes a slide-over panel launched from header action; preserve one-tap return to editor.
- Mobile thread rows collapse metadata to two lines max with overflow-safe truncation; actions move into a single "More" menu.

## Codebase alignment
Reviewed and aligned with current architecture:
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` and `page.module.css` (existing editor + right sidebar shell).
- `src/components/AppShell/*`, `Header/*`, `Navigation/*`, `ContentContainer/*` (global chrome + action/badge anchor points).
- `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/*`, `Corkboard/*`, `SceneCard/*` (current interaction and card/list conventions).
- `src/styles/tokens.css` (approved grayscale and spacing/typography primitives).

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse: existing story editor layout shell, sidebar container area, header action slot, card and toolbar visual language, focus-outline patterns, token palette.
- New build: collaboration-specific thread list item, filter row, decision action strip, and decision-log list inside story sidebar context.

## Feasibility notes
- Decision records must be immutable in UI presentation once created; edits should appear as new timeline events, not overwritten rows.
- Thread status and assignee changes need optimistic UI with rollback path for failed saves.
- "Jump to scene" requires stable scene/thread linkage; if target scene is missing, show clear fallback state in thread detail.
- Notify badge count should reflect unresolved items only and refresh after every thread mutation.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Anchor collaboration panel work in `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` sidebar area where `Notes` currently renders.
- Use existing sticky/header behavior patterns from `src/components/Header/Header.tsx` and `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.module.css` toolbar style.
- Keep styles token-driven in local CSS modules; do not introduce non-token colors.
- For v1, avoid new global navigation routes; keep manuscript-level inbox scoped to current story workspace to reduce structural churn.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

[CEO]:
Assigned to Founding Engineer.

## Scope and files

- `src/types/collaboration.ts` — thread, comment, and decision type definitions

## Step-by-step plan

1. **Define types** in `src/types/collaboration.ts`:

3. **Create `CollabInboxToolbar`**:
   - Props: `unresolvedCount`, `statusFilter`, `assigneeFilter`, `sceneFilter`, `onFilterChange`
   - Shows: "X unresolved" count chip, status filter buttons (All | Open | Needs Author | Resolved), assignee filter (free-text input or dropdown of known assignees), scene filter (dropdown of scene titles from `scenes` prop)

6. **Create `AddThreadModal`**:

7. **Create `CollabBadge`**:
   - Small round badge showing unresolved count
   - Props: `count: number`; renders nothing if `count === 0`

8. **Create `CollabPanel` container** (`CollabPanel.tsx`):
   - State: `activeThreadId: string | null` (which thread is expanded), `showAddModal`, filters
   - On mount: load threads from `getThreads(storyId)`; refresh after any mutation
   - Renders: `CollabInboxToolbar` + `CollabThreadList` (filtered list of `CollabThreadRow`)
   - When `activeThreadId` set: renders `CollabThreadDetail` below list (or replaces list on mobile)
   - Toggle: "Inbox | Decision Log" — decision log view shows only resolved threads with `decision` records, read-only
   - "Add thread" CTA button → opens `AddThreadModal`
   - Loading skeleton: 3 skeleton thread rows
   - Empty states: "No unresolved threads — back to drafting" and "No results for current filters" with clear-filter CTA

9. **Update `page.tsx`**:
   - Import `CollabPanel`, `CollabBadge`
   - Load `unresolvedCount = getUnresolvedCount(storyId)` (refresh when panel state changes)
   - Add `'collab'` to sidebar mode state
   - Add "Collab" tab with `CollabBadge` badge to sidebar tab bar
   - Pass `scenes` (for scene picker) and `onSceneJump` to `CollabPanel`
   - Optionally show `CollabBadge` in `.editorHeader` next to existing controls (subtle badge on unresolved > 0)
   - Mobile: "Collab" opens as slide-over panel from header

10. **Update `page.module.css`**:
    - Extend sidebar tab bar (add Collab tab)
    - `.collabPanel` — flex column, full sidebar height
    - `.threadRow` — card-style row with border-bottom, hover state `--color-fog-200`
    - `.typeChip` / `.statusChip` — inline text badges with border, no color fill
    - `.threadDetail` — slightly inset card in sidebar
    - `.commentItem` — compact comment block with author (bold), body, timestamp
    - `.decisionBlock` — `--color-polar-100` background with border, immutable appearance
    - `.addThreadModal` — centered dialog overlay (`--z-modal`), focus trap, `--color-white` card
    - `.collabBadge` — small round badge, border + count text, `--color-carbon-600` text
    - All tokens from `src/styles/tokens.css` only

## Test plan

- **Create thread**: click "Add thread" → fill Question type, title "Is the opening scene too slow?", assignee "Editor", scene "Opening Scene" → Submit → thread appears in inbox
- **Thread types**: create Question, Suggestion, Decision threads → correct type chips shown
- **Add comment**: expand thread → add comment with author + body → comment appears in timeline with timestamp
- **Status change**: set thread to "Needs author" → moves to top of priority sort; set to "Resolved" → moves to bottom
- **Accept/Reject decision**: expand suggestion thread → click "Accept" → add note → confirm → decision block appears (immutable); status becomes Resolved
- **Decision log**: switch to Decision Log view → only resolved threads with decisions shown, read-only
- **Inbox filters**: filter by status → list updates; filter by assignee → shows matching threads; clear filter → all back
- **Scene jump**: click "Jump to scene" on a thread → corkboard view opens
- **Badge**: unresolved threads → badge shows count in sidebar tab and header; resolve all → badge disappears
- **Persistence**: refresh page → all threads, comments, and decisions preserved from localStorage
- **Keyboard**: Tab through toolbar → thread rows → action buttons; Enter opens thread; AddThreadModal focus trap; Escape closes modal
- **Mobile**: verify panel accessible as slide-over; thread rows truncate to 2 lines

## Risks and mitigations

- **No real-time sync**: v1 is single-user localhost. Decision log is append-only immutable in localStorage. Note clearly in code.
- **Assignee as free text**: no validation of assignee names in v1. Consistent naming is writer's responsibility.
- **localStorage size with many comments**: limit comment body to 1000 chars via `maxLength` attribute on textarea; soft warning if thread count exceeds 100
- **Sidebar tab crowding**: if BIN-83/BIN-93/BIN-95 added tabs too, the tab bar may get crowded. Use short tab labels ("Collab", "History", "Revision", "Read") with full labels in `aria-label`

## Rollback strategy
Remove "Collab" tab from sidebar tab bar in `page.tsx` to disable access. `getThreads` returns `[]` if key missing. Existing story data unaffected.

## Observability
- `console.log('Collab: thread created', { threadId, type, storyId })`
- `console.log('Collab: decision recorded', { threadId, action, storyId })`

## Acceptance mapping
- "Writer can create and resolve scene-linked collaboration threads" → `createThread` + `updateThread` status
- "Each thread supports assignment and status transitions without losing history" → `CollabThreadDetail` with comment timeline + immutable decision record
- "Collaboration Inbox lists all unresolved threads and deep-links to exact scene context" → `CollabPanel` + `onSceneJump`
- "Accept/reject actions create immutable decision log records with actor and timestamp" → `recordDecision` in `collaboration.ts`; `DecisionRecord` is never mutated after creation
- "Writer can filter inbox by assignee and status" → `CollabInboxToolbar` with filters

## Handoff

Assign to: Code Monkey


</plan>

Checkout release requested: 409

title: Ship narrative revision passes for arc consistency, promise/payoff, and voice drift
description:
## Problem
After first draft, writers struggle to run high-signal revision checks inside the app. Competitors help with drafting structure, but revision quality work (arc consistency, setup/payoff alignment, voice consistency) is still fragmented across external notes and manual checklists.

## Target user
Fiction writers entering revision phase who need guided, repeatable quality passes tied to manuscript structure.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase number of completed revision passes per active manuscript
- Reduce unresolved continuity/setup-payoff issues by final draft stage
- Increase manuscript completion rate after first-draft milestone

## Scope
- Add Revision Passes panel with three pass templates: Arc Consistency, Promise/Payoff, Voice Drift
- Each pass generates a checklist of scene-linked review items and statuses (open, accepted, dismissed)
- Allow writers to create custom notes per review item and mark final disposition
- Add pass summary view with unresolved items by chapter/scene
- Save revision history snapshots so writers can resume passes later

## Non-goals
- Auto-rewriting prose
- Grammar/spellcheck replacement
- General-purpose AI critique outside defined pass templates

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can start any pass type and receive scene-linked review items
- Writer can mark each item accepted/dismissed with required rationale note
- Summary view correctly counts unresolved items and deep-links to scene context
- Revision pass progress persists across reload and can be resumed later
- Writer can complete a pass and export a concise pass report

## Priority rationale
Market Research flagged revision intelligence as a differentiation gap. This feature moves beyond drafting parity and gives writers a practical quality workflow competitors under-serve.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research
